HPA should scale\" }\n3698\t\n3699\t- alert: MiroirRequestQueueBacklog"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"25a6aded-57af-4ac0-a003-96233479a803","uuid":"f5631390-ff08-448a-a1b3-80411711be34","timestamp":"2026-05-23T03:31:49.604Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/docs/plan/plan.md","content":"\nAdjust `minReplicas`/`maxReplicas` per the workload tier (§14.7). Metric-type choice matters:\n\n- `miroir_requests_in_flight` is **per-pod** (each pod reports its own in-flight count) and uses `type: Pods` with `AverageValue` — HPA averages across pods and scales to keep the per-pod average at target.\n- `miroir_background_queue_depth` is **global** (every pod reads the same value from the shared Redis `miroir:jobs:_queued` set) and therefore must use `type: External` with `type: Value` — dividing the single global backlog by a per-replica work-unit target. Using `type: Pods`/`AverageValue` here would scale monotonically to `maxReplicas` whenever any backlog exists, because the average never drops as pods are added.\n\nExposing both metrics to the HPA requires `prometheus-adapter` (or equivalent custom-/external-metrics API bridge), which is a chart prerequisite when the HPA is enabled.\n\nWith `replicas >= 2`, the task store backend **must** be Redis (Section 4 `task_store.backend: redis`). SQLite is single-writer and cannot be shared. The Helm chart enforces this: `taskStore.backend=sqlite` with `miroir.replicas > 1` fails values-schema validation.\n\n### 14.5 Horizontal scaling: background work\n\nBackground work must be partitioned, never duplicated across pods. Three coordination modes cover all background tasks:\n\n**Mode A — Shard-partitioned ownership (parallel by shard).**\n\nEach pod owns a subset of shards for background processing. Assignment uses rendezvous hashing over the current peer set:\n\n```\npeers      = discover_peers()           // headless-Service DNS lookup\nowns(s, p) = p == top1_by_score(hash(s || pid) for pid in peers)\n```\n\nApplies to:\n- Anti-entropy reconciler (§13.8) — each pod fingerprints and repairs the shards it owns\n- Settings drift check (§13.5) — each pod polls a subset of (index, node) settings-hash pairs\n- Task registry pruner — each pod prunes tasks where it wins the rendezvous score for the task's `miroir_id`: `top1_by_score(hash(miroir_id || pid) for pid in peers) == self_pid`. Matches anti-entropy / drift-check partitioning; minimal reshuffling on scale events.\n\nWhen the peer set changes (scale event, pod restart), rendezvous redistributes ownership with minimal reshuffling. No explicit handoff — the new owner runs the next scheduled pass. Transient double-work during a 15-second discovery window is harmless: anti-entropy is idempotent, settings-repair is idempotent.\n\n**Mode B — Leader-only (singleton coordinator).**\n\nExactly one pod holds the leader lease at a time, backed by a lease row in the task store (Redis `SET NX EX 10` renewed every 3s; SQLite advisory lock for single-replica deployments). The leader runs operations that must be singleton or atomic:\n\n- Reshard coordinator (§13.1) — one reshard per index at a time; cutover is atomic\n- Rebalancer (existing Section 4) — already uses advisory lock\n- Alias flip serializer (§13.7) — atomic per alias\n- Two-phase settings broadcast (§13.5) — one settings change in flight per index\n\nLeader loss mid-operation causes a pause; the new leader reads the persisted phase state from the task store and resumes from the last committed phase. All operations are idempotent by design and safe to resume at any phase boundary.\n\n**Mode C — Work-queued (streaming jobs that outgrow a single pod).**\n\nHeavy streaming operations — large dump imports (§13.9), large reshard backfills (§13.1) — can exceed a single pod's envelope. They are submitted as **jobs** to a queue in the task store; any pod claims a job with lease-based ownership:\n\n```\njob = {\n    id: uuid,\n    type: \"dump_import\" | \"reshard_backfill\",\n    params: { ... },\n    state: \"queued\" | \"in_progress\" | \"completed\" | \"failed\",\n    claimed_by: pod_id | null,\n    claim_expires_at: ts,              // heartbeat every 10s, timeout 30s\n    progress: { bytes_processed, docs_routed, last_cursor, ... },\n}\n```\n\nA large dump import is **split into chunks** on NDJSON line boundaries by the first pod that picks it up; chunks are re-enqueued as independent jobs. Each chunk is bounded by `dump_import.chunk_size_bytes` (default 256 MiB) so one chunk fits a pod's buffer. HPA reacts to queue depth: if `miroir_background_queue_depth > 0` and pods are at capacity, add pods; once queue drains, scale back down.\n\nReshard backfill partitions by shard-id range and uses the same chunked-job mechanism. Progress cursors are persisted per chunk so a crashed claim resumes at the last committed offset (idempotent via primary keys).\n\n**Peer discovery.** All three modes rely on the current peer set. Mechanism:\n\n- Kubernetes Downward API injects `POD_NAME` and `POD_IP` as env vars\n- A headless Service (`miroir-headless`) with label selector on the Deployment exposes pod IPs via DNS SRV records\n- Each pod refreshes its peer set every 15s via SRV lookup\n- No external service registry required; no Kubernetes API calls from the pod\n\n### 14.6 Per-feature scaling behavior\n\n| Capability | Scaling mode | Notes |\n|------------|-------------|-------|\n| §13.1 Online resharding | B (leader) + C (backfill queue) | Leader owns phase state machine; any pod consumes backfill chunks. Bounded-memory backfill via paginated `filter=_miroir_shard={id}`. |\n| §13.2 Hedged requests | stateless per-request | No coordination needed — each pod hedges its own requests. |\n| §13.3 Adaptive replica selection | per-pod EWMA | Each pod's scores are local; pods converge independently. Slight divergence is harmless. |\n| §13.4 Shard-aware query planner | per-request | Pure function of filter. Plan cache is per-pod. |\n| §13.5 Two-phase settings broadcast | B (leader) | Leader issues PATCH and verifies. Drift reconciler runs in mode A. |\n| §13.6 Session pinning | shared-state per-pod cache | Session row lives in task store (Redis); per-pod LRU caches it. Any pod can serve a session. |\n| §13.7 Atomic index aliases | shared state | Alias table in task store. All pods read same table with short TTL cache. |\n| §13.8 Anti-entropy reconciler | A (shard-partitioned) | Each pod fingerprints its owned shards. Naturally horizontal. |\n| §13.9 Streaming dump import | C (chunked jobs) | 500 GB dump → chunks → pods consume from queue; HPA scales on queue depth. |\n| §13.10 Idempotency + coalescing | per-pod + shared fallback | Idempotency cache per-pod with task-store lookup on miss — a retry on a different pod still dedups. Coalescing is per-pod only (acceptable: identical concurrent queries hitting different pods each issue their own scatter, which is rare and bounded by pod count). |\n| §13.11 Multi-search | stateless per-request | Sub-queries fan out using existing scatter infrastructure; each sub-query is independently routed. |\n| §13.12 Vector / hybrid search | stateless per-request | Merger uses more memory per request (see §14.2 vector over-fetch scratch row); no cross-pod coordination. |\n| §13.13 CDC publisher | per-pod publishers with shared cursors | `cdc_cursors` in the task store serialize cursor advancement via compare-and-swap; each pod publishes its own shard of events. Overflow buffer in Redis is shared across pods. |\n| §13.14 TTL sweeper | A (shard-partitioned) | Each pod sweeps only its rendezvous-owned shards; no duplicate deletes across pods. |\n| §13.15 Tenant affinity | stateless per-request | Hash-or-explicit routing decision; no shared state on the hot path (tenant map LRU is per-pod). |\n| §13.16 Shadow tee | stateless per-request | Each pod independently decides (per its local `sample_rate` RNG) whether to shadow a given request. |\n| §13.17 ILM rollover | B (leader-only) | Serialized alias flips + index create/delete; exactly one pod runs the daily policy evaluator at a time. |\n| §13.18 Canary runner | A (shard-partitioned) | Each canary ID is rendezvous-owned by exactly one pod per interval; no duplicate canary runs. |\n| §13.19 Admin UI | per-pod | Any pod serves the SPA; stateful sections read the shared task store. |\n| §13.20 Explain API | stateless per-request | Pure function of request + topology + config; no cross-pod coordination. |\n| §13.21 Search UI | per-pod (SPA + static assets); rate limiter needs shared state | Any pod serves the SPA. **Rate-limiter requirement:** multi-pod deployments MUST set `search_ui.rate_limit.backend: redis` — `values.schema.json` rejects `backend: local` when `miroir.replicas > 1`. With `backend: local`, the effective cluster-wide rate is `per_ip × pod_count` because each pod counts independently. Redis rate-limit bucket memory sized at ~20 MB per 10k active IPs (§14.7). |\n\n**Note.** TTL sweeper (§13.14), CDC publisher (§13.13), and canary runner (§13.18) are all Mode-A partitioned across pods; each pod consumes only its share of the workload, so the §14.2 memory rows for these features scale with `1/pod_count`.\n\n### 14.7 Revised deployment sizing matrix\n\nEach row gives the orchestrator pod count (2 vCPU / 3.75 GB each) for the stated workload. Meilisearch node sizing follows Section 6 independently.\n\n| Corpus | Peak QPS | Orchestrator pods | Task store |\n|--------|----------|-------------------|------------|\n| ≤ 10 GB | ≤ 500 | 2 (HA) | Redis (or SQLite if replicas=1) |\n| ≤ 50 GB | ≤ 2 k | 2–4 (HPA) | Redis |\n| ≤ 200 GB | ≤ 5 k | 4–8 (HPA) | Redis |\n| ≤ 1 TB | ≤ 20 k | 8–12 (HPA) | Redis |\n| ≤ 5 TB | ≤ 100 k | 12–24 (HPA) | Redis (clustered or Sentinel) |\n\nOrchestrator count scales with query throughput; Meilisearch node count scales with corpus size and RG/RF. They are orthogonal.\n\n**Task-store / Redis memory accounting.** When Redis is the task store, it also backs shared state for idempotency replay keys, session pinning rows, alias cache, background job queue, leader lease, CDC overflow buffer (when configured), and — new in §13.21 — search UI rate-limit buckets. Add **~20 MB for search UI rate-limit buckets at 10k active IPs** on top of the task-store baseline when `search_ui.rate_limit.backend: redis`. Bucket rows auto-expire per `redis_ttl_s` so the footprint stays bounded under IP-scan/spray attacks.\n\n### 14.8 Resource-aware configuration defaults\n\nEvery resource-sensitive knob has a default sized for the 2 vCPU / 3.75 GB envelope:\n\n```yaml\nmiroir:\n  server:\n    max_body_bytes: 104857600          # 100 MiB per request\n    max_concurrent_requests: 500\n    request_timeout_ms: 30000\n\n  connection_pool_per_node:\n    max_idle: 32\n    max_total: 128\n    idle_timeout_s: 60\n\n  task_registry:\n    cache_size: 10000\n    redis_pool_max: 50\n\n  idempotency:\n    max_cached_keys: 1000000           # ~100 MB\n    ttl_seconds: 86400\n\n  session_pinning:\n    max_sessions: 100000               # ~50 MB\n\n  query_coalescing:\n    max_subscribers: 1000\n    max_pending_queries: 10000\n\n  # dump_import: see §13.9 for the full schema. Key horizontal-scaling knob\n  # surfaced here is chunk_size_bytes (256 MiB, fits one pod's buffer) — the\n  # chunk-parallel coordinator in §14.5 Mode C shards work at this granularity.\n\n  anti_entropy:\n    max_read_concurrency: 2\n    fingerprint_batch_size: 1000\n\n  resharding:\n    backfill_concurrency: 4\n    backfill_batch_size: 1000\n\n  peer_discovery:\n    service_name: \"miroir-headless\"\n    refresh_interval_s: 15\n\n  leader_election:\n    enabled: true                      # auto-true when replicas > 1\n    lease_ttl_s: 10\n    renew_interval_s: 3\n```\n\nKubernetes pod resource requests/limits:\n\n```yaml\nresources:\n  requests:\n    cpu: \"500m\"\n    memory: \"1Gi\"\n  limits:\n    cpu: \"2000m\"\n    memory: \"3584Mi\"                   # 3.5 GiB (leaves headroom under 3.75 GB node limit)\n```\n\n### 14.9 Resource-pressure metrics and alerts\n\nNew metrics to surface when a pod is near its envelope or the cluster is under-scaled:\n\n```\nmiroir_memory_pressure             gauge  0=ok, 1=warn (>75%), 2=critical (>90%)\nmiroir_cpu_throttled_seconds_total counter  cgroup CPU throttling\nmiroir_request_queue_depth         gauge  pending requests\nmiroir_background_queue_depth{job_type}  gauge  per job type\nmiroir_peer_pod_count              gauge  observed peer set size\nmiroir_leader                      gauge  1 if this pod is leader, 0 otherwise\nmiroir_owned_shards_count          gauge  shards this pod owns (mode A)\n```\n\nAlerts:\n\n```yaml\n- alert: MiroirMemoryPressure\n  expr: miroir_memory_pressure >= 2\n  for: 5m\n  annotations: { summary: \"Pod near memory limit; HPA should scale\" }\n\n- alert: MiroirRequestQueueBacklog","numLines":200,"startLine":3500,"totalLines":3756}}}

P5.5 §13.5 Two-phase settings broadcast + drift reconciler tests.\n//!\n//! Tests:\n//! - Normal flow: add a synonym; both propose + verify succeed; settings_version increments\n//! - Mid-broadcast node failure: phase 2 verify fails on one node → reissue succeeds after backoff\n//! - Out-of-band drift: PATCH a node directly → drift reconciler detects within interval_s and repairs\n//! - X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version floor excludes stale nodes from covering set; returns 503 when no floor-satisfying covering set exists\n//! - Legacy strategy: sequential still works for rollback compatibility\n\nuse miroir_core::config::MiroirConfig;\nuse miroir_core::settings::{SettingsBroadcast, BroadcastPhase};\nuse miroir_core::task_store::{TaskStore, SqliteTaskStore};\nuse serde_json::json;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\n\n/// Helper to create an in-memory task store for testing.\nfn create_test_task_store() -> Arc<SqliteTaskStore> {\n    Arc::new(SqliteTaskStore::open_in_memory().unwrap())\n}\n\n/// Test 1: Normal flow - add a synonym, both propose + verify succeed, settings_version increments.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_two_phase_settings_broadcast_normal_flow() {\n    let store = create_test_task_store();\n    store.migrate().unwrap();\n\n    let broadcast = SettingsBroadcast::with_task_store(store.clone());\n\n    let index = \"products\".to_string();\n    let settings = json!({\n        \"synonyms\": {\n            \"wifi\": [\"wi-fi\", \"wireless internet\"]\n        }\n    });\n\n    // Start propose phase\n    broadcast.start_propose(index.clone(), &settings).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Enter verify phase with node task UIDs\n    let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n    node_tasks.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), 100);\n    node_tasks.insert(\"node-2\".to_string(), 101);\n    broadcast.enter_verify(&index, node_tasks).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Verify hashes - all nodes should have the same hash\n    let expected_fingerprint = miroir_core::settings::fingerprint_settings(&settings);\n    let mut node_hashes = HashMap::new();\n    node_hashes.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), expected_fingerprint.clone());\n    node_hashes.insert(\"node-2\".to_string(), expected_fingerprint.clone());\n    broadcast.verify_hashes(&index, node_hashes, &expected_fingerprint).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Commit phase - should increment settings version\n    let new_version = broadcast.commit(&index).await.unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(new_version, 1, \"settings_version should be 1 after first commit\");\n\n    // Complete the broadcast\n    broadcast.complete(&index).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Verify node versions are tracked\n    assert_eq!(broadcast.node_version(&index, \"node-1\").await, 1);\n    assert_eq!(broadcast.node_version(&index, \"node-2\").await, 1);\n}\n\n/// Test 2: Hash mismatch with retry - simulate mismatch then successful re-verify.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_two_phase_settings_broadcast_hash_mismatch_retry() {\n    let store = create_test_task_store();\n    store.migrate().unwrap();\n\n    let broadcast = SettingsBroadcast::with_task_store(store.clone());\n\n    let index = \"products\".to_string();\n    let settings = json!({\n        \"rankingRules\": [\"words\", \"typo\"]\n    });\n\n    // Start propose phase\n    broadcast.start_propose(index.clone(), &settings).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Enter verify phase\n    let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n    node_tasks.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), 100);\n    broadcast.enter_verify(&index, node_tasks).await.unwrap();\n\n    let expected_fingerprint = miroir_core::settings::fingerprint_settings(&settings);\n\n    // First verify attempt - node-1 has wrong hash\n    let mut node_hashes = HashMap::new();\n    node_hashes.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), \"wrong_hash\".to_string());\n\n    let result = broadcast.verify_hashes(&index, node_hashes.clone(), &expected_fingerprint).await;\n    assert!(result.is_err(), \"verify should fail with hash mismatch\");\n\n    // Check status reflects the error\n    let status = broadcast.get_status(&index).await;\n    assert!(status.unwrap().error.is_some());\n\n    // Simulate re-issue with correct hash\n    let mut node_hashes = HashMap::new();\n    node_hashes.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), expected_fingerprint.clone());\n\n    broadcast.verify_hashes(&index, node_hashes, &expected_fingerprint).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Commit should succeed\n    let new_version = broadcast.commit(&index).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(new_version, 1);\n}\n\n/// Test 3: Node settings version tracking across multiple updates.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_node_settings_version_tracking_multiple_updates() {\n    let store = create_test_task_store();\n    store.migrate().unwrap();\n\n    let broadcast = SettingsBroadcast::with_task_store(store.clone());\n\n    let index = \"products\".to_string();\n\n    // First settings update\n    let settings1 = json!({\"rankingRules\": [\"words\"]});\n    let node_tasks.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), 100);\n    broadcast.enter_verify(&index, node_tasks).await.unwrap();\n\n    let mut node_hashes = HashMap::new();\n assert_eq!(v1, 1);\n    broadcast.complete(&index).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Second settings update\n    let settings2 = json!({\"rankingRules\": [\"words\", \"typo\"]});\n    let fp2 = node_hashes.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), fp2.clone());\n    broadcast.verify_hashes(&index, node_hashes, &fp2).await.unwrap();\n\n    let v2 = broadcast.commit(&index).await.unwrap();\n version meets floor\n    assert!(broadcast.node_version_meets_floor(&index, \"node-1\", 1).await);\n    assert!(broadcast.node_version_meets_floor(&index, \"node-2\", 0).await);\n    assert!(!broadcast.node_version_meets_floor(&index, \"node-1\", 2).await);\n}\n\n/// Test 5: Settings persistence to task store.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn *self.group_totals.entry(group_id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n96\t    }\n97\t\n98\t    /// Record a successful write ACK from a node.\n99\t    fn record_success(&mut self, group_id: u32, _node_id: &NodeId) {\n100\t        *self.group_acks.entry(group_id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n101\t    }\n102\t\n103\t    /// Record a failed write attempt from a node.\n104\t    fn record_failure(&mut self, _group_id: u32) {\n105\t        // Track that this group had a failure\n106\t        // Degraded is determined after checking quorum\n107\t    }\n108\t\n109\t    /// Check if a group has met quorum: floor(RF/2) + 1 ACKs required.\n110\t    fn check_group_quorum(&mut self, group_id: u32, rf: usize) -> bool {\n111\t        let acks = *self.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n112\t        let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n113\t        let met = acks >= quorum;\n114\t        *self.groups_met_quorum.entry(group_id).or_insert(false) = met;\n115\t        met\n116\t    }\n117\t\n118\t    /// Count how many groups met quorum.\n119\t    fn count_quorum_groups(&self) -> usize {\n120\t        self.groups_met_quorum.values().filter(|&&v| v).count()\n121\t    }\n122\t\n123\t    /// Count degraded groups (groups that exist but didn't meet quorum).\n124\t    fn count_degraded_groups(&mut self, replica_group_count: u32, rf: usize) -> u32 {\n125\t        let mut degraded = 0u32;\n126\t        for group_id in 0..replica_group_count {\n127\t            if !self.check_group_quorum(group_id, rf) {\n128\t                // Only count as degraded if we attempted to write to this group\n129\t                if self.group_totals.contains_key(&group_id) {\n130\t                    degraded += 1;\n131\t                }\n132\t            }\n133\t        }\n134\t        degraded\n135\t    }\n136\t}\n137\t\n138\t/// Build router for document endpoints.\n139\tpub fn router<S>() -> Router<S>\n140\twhere\n141\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n142\t{\n143\t    Router::new()\n144\t        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::post(post_documents))\n145\t        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::put(put_documents))\n146\t        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::delete(delete_documents))\n147\t        .route(\"/:id\", axum::routing::delete(delete_document_by_id))\n148\t}\n149\t\n150\t/// POST /indexes/{uid}/documents - Add documents.\n151\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\n152\tasync fn post_documents(\n153\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n154\t    Query(params): Query<DocumentsParams>,\n155\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n156\t    Json(documents): Json<Vec<Value>>,\n157\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n158\t    // Extract session ID from request extensions (set by session_pinning_middleware)\n159\t    let session_id = crate::middleware::SessionId::default(); // Will be overridden by middleware\n160\t\n161\t    write_documents_impl(index, params.primaryKey, documents, &state, None).await\n162\t}\n163\t\n164\t/// PUT /indexes/{uid}/documents - Replace documents.\n165\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\n166\tasync fn put_documents(\n167\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n168\t    Query(params): Query<DocumentsParams>,\n169\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n170\t    Json(documents): Json<Vec<Value>>,\n171\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n172\t    write_documents_impl(index, params.primaryKey, documents, &state, None).await\n173\t}\n174\t\n175\t/// DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents - Delete by IDs or filter.\n176\tasync fn delete_documents(\n177\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n178\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n179\t    Json(body): Json<Value>,\n180\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n181\t    // Try to parse as delete by filter first\n182\t    if let Some(filter) = body.get(\"filter\") {\n183\t        let req = DeleteByFilterRequest {\n184\t            index_uid: index.clone(),\n185\t            filter: filter.clone(),\n186\t        };\n187\t        return delete_by_filter_impl(index, req, &state).await;\n188\t    }\n189\t\n190\t    // Try to parse as delete by IDs\n191\t    if let Some(ids) = body.get(\"ids\").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {\n192\t        let ids: Vec<String> = ids\n193\t            .iter()\n194\t            .filter_map(|v| v.as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n195\t            .collect();\n196\t        if !ids.is_empty() {\n197\t            let req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n198\t                index_uid: index.clone(),\n199\t                ids,\n200\t            };\n201\t            return delete_by_ids_impl(index, req, &state).await;\n202\t        }\n203\t    }\n204\t\n205\t    // If we get here, the request body is malformed\n206\t    Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n207\t        MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n208\t        \"delete request must include either 'filter' or 'ids' field\",\n209\t    ))\n210\t}\n211\t\n212\t/// DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents/{id} - Delete single document by ID.\n213\tasync fn delete_document_by_id(\n214\t    Path((index, id)): Path<(String, String)>,\n215\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n216\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n217\t    let req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n218\t        index_uid: index.clone(),\n219\t        ids: vec![id],\n220\t    };\n221\t    delete_by_ids_impl(index, req, &state).await\n222\t}\n223\t\n224\t/// Implementation for write documents (POST/PUT).\n225\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index, session_id))]\n226\tasync fn write_documents_impl(\n227\t    index: String,\n228\t    primary_key: Option<String>,\n229\t    mut documents: Vec<Value>,\n230\t    state: &AppState,\n231\t    session_id: Option<String>,\n232\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n233\t    if documents.is_empty() {\n234\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n235\t            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n236\t            \"cannot write empty document batch\",\n237\t        ));\n238\t    }\n239\t\n240\t    // 1. Extract primary key from first document if not provided\n241\t    let primary_key = primary_key.or_else(|| {\n242\t        documents\n243\t            .first()\n244\t            .and_then(|doc| extract_primary_key(doc))\n245\t    });\n246\t\n247\t    let primary_key = primary_key.ok_or_else(|| {\n248\t        MeilisearchError::new(\n249\t            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n250\t            format!(\"primary key required for index `{}`\", index),\n251\t        )\n252\t    })?;\n253\t\n254\t    // 2. Validate all documents have the primary key and check for reserved field\n255\t    let anti_entropy_enabled = state.config.anti_entropy.enabled;\n256\t    let updated_at_field = &state.config.anti_entropy.updated_at_field;\n257\t    let ttl_enabled = state.config.ttl.enabled;\n258\t    let expires_at_field = &state.config.ttl.expires_at_field;\n259\t\n260\t    for (i, doc) in documents.iter().enumerate() {\n261\t        // Check for reserved field BEFORE checking primary key (per acceptance criteria)\n262\t        // _miroir_shard is ALWAYS reserved (plan §5)\n263\t        if doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some() {\n264\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n265\t                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n266\t                \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_shard`\",\n267\t            ));\n268\t        }\n269\t\n270\t        // _miroir_updated_at is reserved ONLY when anti_entropy.enabled: true (plan §5, §13.8)\n271\t        if anti_entropy_enabled && doc.get(updated_at_field).is_some() {\n272\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n273\t                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n274\t                format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when anti_entropy.enabled: true)\", updated_at_field),\n275\t            ));\n276\t        }\n277\t\n278\t        // _miroir_expires_at is reserved ONLY when ttl.enabled: true (plan §5, §13.14)\n279\t        // When reserved, clients cannot SET it; the orchestrator controls it. When disabled,\n280\t        // client values pass through end-to-end.\n281\t        if ttl_enabled && doc.get(expires_at_field).is_some() {\n282\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n283\t                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n284\t                format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when ttl.enabled: true)\", expires_at_field),\n285\t            ));\n286\t        }\n287\t\n288\t        if doc.get(&primary_key).is_none() {\n289\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n290\t                MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n291\t                format!(\n292\t                    \"document at index {} missing primary key field `{}`\",\n293\t                    i, primary_key\n294\t                ),\n295\t            ));\n296\t        }\n297\t    }\n298\t\n299\t    // 3. Inject _miroir_shard into each document\n300\t    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n301\t    let shard_count = topology.shards;\n302\t    let rf = topology.rf();\n303\t    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n304\t\n305\t    for doc in &mut documents {\n306\t        if let Some(pk_value) = doc.get(&primary_key).and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {\n307\t            let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, shard_count);\n308\t            doc[\"_miroir_shard\"] = serde_json::json!(shard_id);\n309\t        }\n310\t    }\n311\t\n312\t    // 4. Group documents by target nodes (per-batch grouping for efficient fan-out)\n313\t    let node_documents = group_documents_by_shard(&documents, &primary_key, &topology)?;\n314\t\n315\t    // 5. Fan out to nodes and track quorum\n316\t    let client = HttpClient::new(\n317\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n318\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n319\t    );\n320\t\n321\t    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n322\t    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n323\t\n324\t    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group (with dual-write support)\n325\t    for (shard_id, docs) in node_documents {\n326\t        // Get migration coordinator reference for dual-write detection\n327\t        let migration_coordinator = state.migration_coordinator.as_ref().map(|c| {\n328\t            // We need a read lock on the coordinator\n329\t            tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n330\t                tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n331\t                    c.read().await\n332\t                })\n333\t            })\n334\t        });\n335\t\n336\t        // Use migration-aware routing\n337\t        let targets = write_targets_with_migration(\n338\t            shard_id,\n339\t            &topology,\n340\t            migration_coordinator.as_deref(),\n341\t        );\n342\t\n343\t        if targets.is_empty() {\n344\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n345\t                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n346\t                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n347\t            ));\n348\t        }\n349\t\n350\t        // Track which groups we're targeting for this shard\n351\t\n352\t        for node_id in targets {\n353\t            let node = topology\n354\t                .node(&node_id)\n355\t                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n356\t\n357\t            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n358\t            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n359\t\n360\t            let req = WriteRequest {\n361\t                index_uid: index.clone(),\n362\t                documents: docs.clone(),\n363\t                primary_key: Some(primary_key.clone()),\n364\t            };\n365\t\n366\t            match client.write_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n367\t                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n368\t                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n369\t                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n370\t                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n371\t                    }\n372\t                }\n373\t                Ok(resp) => {\n374\t                    // Non-success response (validation error, etc.)\n375\t                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n376\t                }\n377\t                Err(_) => {\n378\t                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n379\t                }\n380\t            }\n381\t        }\n382\t    }\n383\t\n384\t    // 6. Apply two-rule quorum logic\n385\t    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n386\t    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n387\t\n388\t    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n389\t    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n390\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n391\t            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n392\t            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n393\t        ));\n394\t    }\n395\t\n396\t    // 6.5. Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n397\t    // Groups are checked in ascending order, so the first one with quorum is the first\n398\t    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n399\t        .find(|&group_id| {\n400\t            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n401\t            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n402\t            acks >= quorum\n403\t        })\n404\t        .unwrap_or(0); // Default to group 0 if somehow no quorum (shouldn't happen here)\n405\t\n406\t    // 7. Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n407\t    let miroir_task = state\n408\t        .task_registry\n409\t        .register_with_metadata(\n410\t            node_task_uids.clone(),\n411\t            Some(index.clone()),\n412\t            Some(\"documentAdditionOrUpdate\".to_string()),\n413\t        )\n414\t        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n415\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n416\t            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n417\t        ))?;\n418\t\n419\t    // 7.5. Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n420\t    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n421\t        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n422\t            sid,\n423\t            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n424\t            first_quorum_group,\n425\t        ).await {\n426\t            // Log error but don't fail the write - session pinning is best-effort\n427\t            tracing::error!(\n428\t                session_id = %sid,\n429\t                error = %e,\n430\t                \"failed to record session pinning for write\"\n431\t            );\n432\t        }\n433\t    }\n434\t\n435\t    // Build success response with degraded header and mtask ID\n436\t    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n437\t        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n438\t            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n439\t            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n440\t            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n441\t            error: None,\n442\t            error_type: None,\n443\t            code: None,\n444\t            link: None,\n445\t        },\n446\t        degraded_groups,\n447\t    )\n448\t}\n449\t\n450\t/// Implementation for delete by IDs.\n451\tasync fn delete_by_ids_impl(\n452\t    index: String,\n453\t    req: DeleteByIdsRequest,\n454\t    state: &AppState,\n455\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n456\t    if req.ids.is_empty() {\n457\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n458\t            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n459\t            \"cannot delete empty ID list\",\n460\t        ));\n461\t    }\n462\t\n463\t    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n464\t    let rf = topology.rf();\n465\t    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n466\t\n467\t    // Group IDs by target shard for independent per-shard routing\n468\t    let mut shard_ids: HashMap<u32, Vec<String>> = HashMap::new();\n469\t    for id in &req.ids {\n470\t        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, topology.shards);\n471\t        shard_ids.entry(shard_id).or_default().push(id.clone());\n472\t    }\n473\t\n474\t    let client = HttpClient::new(\n475\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n476\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n477\t    );\n478\t\n479\t    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n480\t    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n481\t\n482\t    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group\n483\t    for (shard_id, ids) in shard_ids {\n484\t        let targets = miroir_core::router::write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n485\t\n486\t        if targets.is_empty() {\n487\t            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n488\t                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n489\t                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n490\t            ));\n491\t        }\n492\t\n493\t        for node_id in targets {\n494\t            let node = topology\n495\t                .node(&node_id)\n496\t                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n497\t\n498\t            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n499\t            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n500\t\n501\t            let delete_req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n502\t                index_uid: index.clone(),\n503\t                ids: ids.clone(),\n504\t            };\n505\t\n506\t            match client.delete_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &delete_req).await {\n507\t                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n508\t                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n509\t                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n510\t                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n511\t                    }\n512\t                }\n513\t                Ok(resp) => {\n514\t                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n515\t                }\n516\t                Err(_) => {\n517\t                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n518\t                }\n519\t            }\n520\t        }\n521\t    }\n522\t\n523\t    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n524\t    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n525\t    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n526\t\n527\t    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n528\t    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n529\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n530\t            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n531\t            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n532\t        ));\n533\t    }\n534\t\n535\t    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n536\t    let miroir_task = state\n537\t        .task_registry\n538\t        .register_with_metadata(\n539\t            node_task_uids.clone(),\n540\t            Some(index.clone()),\n541\t            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n542\t        )\n543\t        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n544\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n545\t            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n546\t        ))?;\n547\t\n548\t    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n549\t        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n550\t            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n551\t            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n552\t            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n553\t            error: None,\n554\t            error_type: None,\n555\t            code: None,\n556\t            link: None,\n557\t        },\n558\t        degraded_groups,\n559\t    )\n560\t}\n561\t\n562\t/// Implementation for delete by filter (broadcast to all nodes).\n563\tasync fn delete_by_filter_impl(\n564\t    index: String,\n565\t    req: DeleteByFilterRequest,\n566\t    state: &AppState,\n567\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n568\t    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n569\t    let rf = topology.rf();\n570\t    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n571\t\n572\t    let client = HttpClient::new(\n573\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n574\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n575\t    );\n576\t\n577\t    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n578\t    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n579\t\n580\t    // Broadcast to all nodes (cannot shard-route for filters)\n581\t    for node in topology.nodes() {\n582\t        let group_id = node.replica_group;\n583\t        quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node.id);\n584\t\n585\t        match client\n586\t            .delete_documents_by_filter(&node.id, &node.address, &req)\n587\t            .await\n588\t        {\n589\t            Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n590\t                quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node.id);\n591\t                if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n592\t                    node_task_uids.insert(node.id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n593\t                }\n594\t            }\n595\t            Ok(resp) => {\n596\t                return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n597\t            }\n598\t            Err(_) => {\n599\t                quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n600\t            }\n601\t        }\n602\t    }\n603\t\n604\t    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n605\t    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n606\t    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n607\t\n608\t    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n609\t    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n610\t        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n611\t            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n612\t            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n613\t        ));\n614\t    }\n615\t\n616\t    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n617\t    let miroir_task = state\n618\t        .task_registry\n619\t        .register_with_metadata(\n620\t            node_task_uids.clone(),\n621\t            Some(index.clone()),\n622\t            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n623\t        )\n624\t        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n625\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n626\t            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n627\t        ))?;\n628\t\n629\t    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n630\t        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n631\t            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n632\t            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n633\t            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n634\t            error: None,\n635\t            error_type: None,\n636\t            code: None,\n637\t            link: None,\n638\t        },\n639\t        degraded_groups,\n640\t    )\n641\t}\n642\t\n643\t/// Extract primary key from a document by checking common field names.\n644\t///\n645\t/// Tries fields in order: id, pk, key, _id.\n646\tfn extract_primary_key(doc: &Value) -> Option<String> {\n647\t    [\"id\", \"pk\", \"key\", \"_id\"]\n648\t        .iter()\n649\t        .find(|&&key| doc.get(key).is_some())\n650\t        .map(|&s| s.to_string())\n651\t}\n652\t\n653\t/// Group documents by their target shard for fan-out optimization.\n654\t///\n655\t/// Returns a map of shard_id -> documents to send to that shard.\n656\t/// The caller then fans out each shard's documents to all RF nodes in each group.\n657\t///\n658\t/// This per-batch grouping minimizes HTTP fan-out count (critical at scale).\n659\tfn group_documents_by_shard(\n660\t    documents: &[Value],\n661\t    primary_key: &str,\n662\t    topology: &Topology,\n663\t) -> std::result::Result<HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>>, MeilisearchError> {\n664\t    let mut shard_documents: HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>> = HashMap::new();\n665\t\n666\t    for doc in documents {\n667\t        let pk_value = doc\n668\t            .get(primary_key)\n669\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n670\t            .ok_or_else(|| {\n671\t                MeilisearchError::new(\n672\t                    MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n673\t                    \"primary key value must be a string\",\n674\t                )\n675\t            })?;\n676\t\n677\t        let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, topology.shards);\n678\t        shard_documents\n679\t            .entry(shard_id)\n680\t            .or_default()\n681\t            .push(doc.clone());\n682\t    }\n683\t\n684\t    Ok(shard_documents)\n685\t}\n686\t\n687\t/// Build an error response from a node error.\n688\tfn build_error_response(resp: WriteResponse) -> DocumentsWriteResponse {\n689\t    DocumentsWriteResponse {\n690\t        taskUid: resp.task_uid.map(|uid| uid.to_string()),\n691\t        indexUid: None,\n692\t        status: None,\n693\t        error: resp.message,\n694\t        error_type: resp.error_type,\n695\t        code: resp.code,\n696\t        link: None,\n697\t    }\n698\t}\n699\t\n700\t/// Build a success response with optional X-Miroir-Degraded header.\n701\tfn build_response_with_degraded_header(\n702\t    response: DocumentsWriteResponse,\n703\t    degraded_groups: u32,\n704\t) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n705\t    let body = serde_json::to_string(&response).map_err(|e| {\n706\t        MeilisearchError::new(\n707\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n708\t            format!(\"failed to serialize response: {}\", e),\n709\t        )\n710\t    })?;\n711\t\n712\t    let mut builder = Response::builder()\n713\t        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n714\t        .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\");\n715\t\n716\t    // Add X-Miroir-Degraded header if any groups were degraded\n717\t    if degraded_groups > 0 {\n718\t        builder = builder.header(HEADER_MIROIR_DEGRADED, format!(\"groups={}\", degraded_groups));\n719\t    }\n720\t\n721\t    Ok(builder\n722\t        .body(axum::body::Body::from(body))\n723\t        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n724\t            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n725\t            format!(\"failed to build response: {}\", e),\n726\t        ))?)\n727\t}\n728\t\n729\t/// Build an error response as JSON (for forwarded node errors).\n730\tfn build_json_error_response(resp: DocumentsWriteResponse) -> Response {\n731\t    (\n732\t        StatusCode::OK,\n733\t        [(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\")],\n734\t        Json(resp),\n735\t    )\n736\t        .into_response()\n737\t}\n738\t\n739\t#[cfg(test)]\n740\tmod tests {\n741\t    use super::*;\n742\t    use miroir_core::api_error::{MiroirCode, MeilisearchError};\n743\t    use serde_json::json;\n744\t\n745\t    #[test]\n746\t    fn test_extract_primary_key_common_fields() {\n747\t        let doc_with_id = serde_json::json!({\"id\": \"test123\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n748\t }\n1021\t}\n1022\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"25a6aded-57af-4ac0-a003-96233479a803","uuid":"7d03b1bf-0517-443a-99a8-c12e9216fca8","timestamp":"2026-05-23T03:31:57.397Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-proxy/src/routes/documents.rs","content":"//! Document write path: add, replace, and delete documents.\n//!\n//! Implements P2.2 write path:\n//! - Primary key extraction on the hot path\n//! - `_miroir_shard` injection\n//! - Reserved field rejection\n//! - Two-rule quorum\n//!\n//! Implements P2.5 task reconciliation:\n//! - Collects per-node task UIDs\n//! - Registers Miroir task ID (mtask-<uuid>)\n//! - Returns mtask ID to client\n//!\n//! Implements §13.6 session pinning:\n//! - Records writes with session header\n//! - Tracks pinned group (first to reach quorum)\n\nuse axum::extract::{Extension, Path, Query};\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\nuse axum::http::{StatusCode, header};\nuse axum::{Json, Router};\nuse miroir_core::api_error::{MiroirCode, MeilisearchError};\nuse miroir_core::router::{shard_for_key, write_targets_with_migration};\nuse miroir_core::scatter::{DeleteByIdsRequest, DeleteByFilterRequest, NodeClient, WriteRequest, WriteResponse};\nuse miroir_core::session_pinning::SessionId;\nuse miroir_core::task::TaskRegistry;\nuse miroir_core::topology::{Topology, NodeId};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tracing::{debug, info, instrument};\n\nuse crate::client::HttpClient;\nuse crate::routes::admin_endpoints::AppState;\n\n/// Document write parameters from query string.\n#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\npub struct DocumentsParams {\n    primaryKey: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Task response (Meilisearch-compatible).\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct TaskResponse {\n    taskUid: u64,\n    indexUid: String,\n    status: String,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    error: Option<Value>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    error_type: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Response for write operations.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct DocumentsWriteResponse {\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    taskUid: Option<String>, // Changed to String to hold mtask-<uuid>\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    indexUid: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    status: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    error: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    error_type: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    code: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    link: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Header name for degraded write responses.\npub const HEADER_MIROIR_DEGRADED: &str = \"X-Miroir-Degraded\";\n\n/// Quorum tracking state for write operations.\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\nstruct QuorumState {\n    /// Per-group ACK counts: group_id -> successful_ack_count\n    group_acks: HashMap<u32, usize>,\n    /// Per-group total node counts: group_id -> total_nodes_attempted\n    group_totals: HashMap<u32, usize>,\n    /// Groups that met quorum: group_id -> true\n    groups_met_quorum: HashMap<u32, bool>,\n    /// Total degraded groups count\n    degraded_groups: u32,\n}\n\nimpl QuorumState {\n    /// Record a write attempt to a node.\n    fn record_attempt(&mut self, group_id: u32, _node_id: &NodeId) {\n        *self.group_totals.entry(group_id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n    }\n\n    /// Record a successful write ACK from a node.\n    fn record_success(&mut self, group_id: u32, _node_id: &NodeId) {\n        *self.group_acks.entry(group_id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n    }\n\n    /// Record a failed write attempt from a node.\n    fn record_failure(&mut self, _group_id: u32) {\n        // Track that this group had a failure\n        // Degraded is determined after checking quorum\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a group has met quorum: floor(RF/2) + 1 ACKs required.\n    fn check_group_quorum(&mut self, group_id: u32, rf: usize) -> bool {\n        let acks = *self.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n        let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n        let met = acks >= quorum;\n        *self.groups_met_quorum.entry(group_id).or_insert(false) = met;\n        met\n    }\n\n    /// Count how many groups met quorum.\n    fn count_quorum_groups(&self) -> usize {\n        self.groups_met_quorum.values().filter(|&&v| v).count()\n    }\n\n    /// Count degraded groups (groups that exist but didn't meet quorum).\n    fn count_degraded_groups(&mut self, replica_group_count: u32, rf: usize) -> u32 {\n        let mut degraded = 0u32;\n        for group_id in 0..replica_group_count {\n            if !self.check_group_quorum(group_id, rf) {\n                // Only count as degraded if we attempted to write to this group\n                if self.group_totals.contains_key(&group_id) {\n                    degraded += 1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        degraded\n    }\n}\n\n/// Build router for document endpoints.\npub fn router<S>() -> Router<S>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n{\n    Router::new()\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::post(post_documents))\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::put(put_documents))\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::delete(delete_documents))\n        .route(\"/:id\", axum::routing::delete(delete_document_by_id))\n}\n\n/// POST /indexes/{uid}/documents - Add documents.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\nasync fn post_documents(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Query(params): Query<DocumentsParams>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    Json(documents): Json<Vec<Value>>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    // Extract session ID from request extensions (set by session_pinning_middleware)\n    let session_id = crate::middleware::SessionId::default(); // Will be overridden by middleware\n\n    write_documents_impl(index, params.primaryKey, documents, &state, None).await\n}\n\n/// PUT /indexes/{uid}/documents - Replace documents.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\nasync fn put_documents(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Query(params): Query<DocumentsParams>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    Json(documents): Json<Vec<Value>>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    write_documents_impl(index, params.primaryKey, documents, &state, None).await\n}\n\n/// DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents - Delete by IDs or filter.\nasync fn delete_documents(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    Json(body): Json<Value>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    // Try to parse as delete by filter first\n    if let Some(filter) = body.get(\"filter\") {\n        let req = DeleteByFilterRequest {\n            index_uid: index.clone(),\n            filter: filter.clone(),\n        };\n        return delete_by_filter_impl(index, req, &state).await;\n    }\n\n    // Try to parse as delete by IDs\n    if let Some(ids) = body.get(\"ids\").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {\n        let ids: Vec<String> = ids\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|v| v.as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        if !ids.is_empty() {\n            let req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n                index_uid: index.clone(),\n                ids,\n            };\n            return delete_by_ids_impl(index, req, &state).await;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // If we get here, the request body is malformed\n    Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n        MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n        \"delete request must include either 'filter' or 'ids' field\",\n    ))\n}\n\n/// DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents/{id} - Delete single document by ID.\nasync fn delete_document_by_id(\n    Path((index, id)): Path<(String, String)>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n        index_uid: index.clone(),\n        ids: vec![id],\n    };\n    delete_by_ids_impl(index, req, &state).await\n}\n\n/// Implementation for write documents (POST/PUT).\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index, session_id))]\nasync fn write_documents_impl(\n    index: String,\n    primary_key: Option<String>,\n    mut documents: Vec<Value>,\n    state: &AppState,\n    session_id: Option<String>,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    if documents.is_empty() {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n            \"cannot write empty document batch\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // 1. Extract primary key from first document if not provided\n    let primary_key = primary_key.or_else(|| {\n        documents\n            .first()\n            .and_then(|doc| extract_primary_key(doc))\n    });\n\n    let primary_key = primary_key.ok_or_else(|| {\n        MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n            format!(\"primary key required for index `{}`\", index),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    // 2. Validate all documents have the primary key and check for reserved field\n    let anti_entropy_enabled = state.config.anti_entropy.enabled;\n    let updated_at_field = &state.config.anti_entropy.updated_at_field;\n    let ttl_enabled = state.config.ttl.enabled;\n    let expires_at_field = &state.config.ttl.expires_at_field;\n\n    for (i, doc) in documents.iter().enumerate() {\n        // Check for reserved field BEFORE checking primary key (per acceptance criteria)\n        // _miroir_shard is ALWAYS reserved (plan §5)\n        if doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n                \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_shard`\",\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // _miroir_updated_at is reserved ONLY when anti_entropy.enabled: true (plan §5, §13.8)\n        if anti_entropy_enabled && doc.get(updated_at_field).is_some() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n                format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when anti_entropy.enabled: true)\", updated_at_field),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // _miroir_expires_at is reserved ONLY when ttl.enabled: true (plan §5, §13.14)\n        // When reserved, clients cannot SET it; the orchestrator controls it. When disabled,\n        // client values pass through end-to-end.\n        if ttl_enabled && doc.get(expires_at_field).is_some() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n                format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when ttl.enabled: true)\", expires_at_field),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        if doc.get(&primary_key).is_none() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n                format!(\n                    \"document at index {} missing primary key field `{}`\",\n                    i, primary_key\n                ),\n            ));\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 3. Inject _miroir_shard into each document\n    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n    let shard_count = topology.shards;\n    let rf = topology.rf();\n    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n\n    for doc in &mut documents {\n        if let Some(pk_value) = doc.get(&primary_key).and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {\n            let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, shard_count);\n            doc[\"_miroir_shard\"] = serde_json::json!(shard_id);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 4. Group documents by target nodes (per-batch grouping for efficient fan-out)\n    let node_documents = group_documents_by_shard(&documents, &primary_key, &topology)?;\n\n    // 5. Fan out to nodes and track quorum\n    let client = HttpClient::new(\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    );\n\n    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group (with dual-write support)\n    for (shard_id, docs) in node_documents {\n        // Get migration coordinator reference for dual-write detection\n        let migration_coordinator = state.migration_coordinator.as_ref().map(|c| {\n            // We need a read lock on the coordinator\n            tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n                tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(async {\n                    c.read().await\n                })\n            })\n        });\n\n        // Use migration-aware routing\n        let targets = write_targets_with_migration(\n            shard_id,\n            &topology,\n            migration_coordinator.as_deref(),\n        );\n\n        if targets.is_empty() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // Track which groups we're targeting for this shard\n\n        for node_id in targets {\n            let node = topology\n                .node(&node_id)\n                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n\n            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n\n            let req = WriteRequest {\n                index_uid: index.clone(),\n                documents: docs.clone(),\n                primary_key: Some(primary_key.clone()),\n            };\n\n            match client.write_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n                    }\n                }\n                Ok(resp) => {\n                    // Non-success response (validation error, etc.)\n                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n                }\n                Err(_) => {\n                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 6. Apply two-rule quorum logic\n    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n\n    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // 6.5. Find the first group to reach quorum (for session pinning, plan §13.6)\n    // Groups are checked in ascending order, so the first one with quorum is the first\n    let first_quorum_group = (0..replica_group_count)\n        .find(|&group_id| {\n            let acks = *quorum_state.group_acks.get(&group_id).unwrap_or(&0);\n            let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n            acks >= quorum\n        })\n        .unwrap_or(0); // Default to group 0 if somehow no quorum (shouldn't happen here)\n\n    // 7. Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n    let miroir_task = state\n        .task_registry\n        .register_with_metadata(\n            node_task_uids.clone(),\n            Some(index.clone()),\n            Some(\"documentAdditionOrUpdate\".to_string()),\n        )\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n        ))?;\n\n    // 7.5. Record session pinning if session header present (plan §13.6)\n    if let (Some(ref sid), true) = (&session_id, state.session_manager.is_enabled()) {\n        if let Err(e) = state.session_manager.record_write_with_quorum(\n            sid,\n            miroir_task.miroir_id.clone(),\n            first_quorum_group,\n        ).await {\n            // Log error but don't fail the write - session pinning is best-effort\n            tracing::error!(\n                session_id = %sid,\n                error = %e,\n                \"failed to record session pinning for write\"\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Build success response with degraded header and mtask ID\n    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n            error: None,\n            error_type: None,\n            code: None,\n            link: None,\n        },\n        degraded_groups,\n    )\n}\n\n/// Implementation for delete by IDs.\nasync fn delete_by_ids_impl(\n    index: String,\n    req: DeleteByIdsRequest,\n    state: &AppState,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    if req.ids.is_empty() {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n            \"cannot delete empty ID list\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n    let rf = topology.rf();\n    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n\n    // Group IDs by target shard for independent per-shard routing\n    let mut shard_ids: HashMap<u32, Vec<String>> = HashMap::new();\n    for id in &req.ids {\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, topology.shards);\n        shard_ids.entry(shard_id).or_default().push(id.clone());\n    }\n\n    let client = HttpClient::new(\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    );\n\n    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // For each shard, write to all RF nodes in each replica group\n    for (shard_id, ids) in shard_ids {\n        let targets = miroir_core::router::write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n\n        if targets.is_empty() {\n            return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n                format!(\"no available nodes for shard {}\", shard_id),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        for node_id in targets {\n            let node = topology\n                .node(&node_id)\n                .ok_or_else(|| MeilisearchError::new(MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable, \"node not found in topology\"))?;\n\n            let group_id = node.replica_group;\n            quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node_id);\n\n            let delete_req = DeleteByIdsRequest {\n                index_uid: index.clone(),\n                ids: ids.clone(),\n            };\n\n            match client.delete_documents(&node_id, &node.address, &delete_req).await {\n                Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n                    quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node_id);\n                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n                        node_task_uids.insert(node_id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n                    }\n                }\n                Ok(resp) => {\n                    return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n                }\n                Err(_) => {\n                    quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n\n    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n    let miroir_task = state\n        .task_registry\n        .register_with_metadata(\n            node_task_uids.clone(),\n            Some(index.clone()),\n            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n        )\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n        ))?;\n\n    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n            error: None,\n            error_type: None,\n            code: None,\n            link: None,\n        },\n        degraded_groups,\n    )\n}\n\n/// Implementation for delete by filter (broadcast to all nodes).\nasync fn delete_by_filter_impl(\n    index: String,\n    req: DeleteByFilterRequest,\n    state: &AppState,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let topology = state.topology.read().await;\n    let rf = topology.rf();\n    let replica_group_count = topology.replica_group_count();\n\n    let client = HttpClient::new(\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone(),\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    );\n\n    let mut quorum_state = QuorumState::default();\n    let mut node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // Broadcast to all nodes (cannot shard-route for filters)\n    for node in topology.nodes() {\n        let group_id = node.replica_group;\n        quorum_state.record_attempt(group_id, &node.id);\n\n        match client\n            .delete_documents_by_filter(&node.id, &node.address, &req)\n            .await\n        {\n            Ok(resp) if resp.success => {\n                quorum_state.record_success(group_id, &node.id);\n                if let Some(task_uid) = resp.task_uid {\n                    node_task_uids.insert(node.id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n                }\n            }\n            Ok(resp) => {\n                return Ok(build_json_error_response(build_error_response(resp)));\n            }\n            Err(_) => {\n                quorum_state.record_failure(group_id);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Apply two-rule quorum logic\n    let degraded_groups = quorum_state.count_degraded_groups(replica_group_count, rf);\n    let quorum_groups = quorum_state.count_quorum_groups();\n\n    // Write success if at least one group met quorum\n    if quorum_groups == 0 {\n        return Err(MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n            \"no replica group met quorum\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Register Miroir task with collected node task UIDs\n    let miroir_task = state\n        .task_registry\n        .register_with_metadata(\n            node_task_uids.clone(),\n            Some(index.clone()),\n            Some(\"documentDeletion\".to_string()),\n        )\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to register task: {}\", e),\n        ))?;\n\n    build_response_with_degraded_header(\n        DocumentsWriteResponse {\n            taskUid: Some(miroir_task.miroir_id),\n            indexUid: Some(index.clone()),\n            status: Some(\"enqueued\".to_string()),\n            error: None,\n            error_type: None,\n            code: None,\n            link: None,\n        },\n        degraded_groups,\n    )\n}\n\n/// Extract primary key from a document by checking common field names.\n///\n/// Tries fields in order: id, pk, key, _id.\nfn extract_primary_key(doc: &Value) -> Option<String> {\n    [\"id\", \"pk\", \"key\", \"_id\"]\n        .iter()\n        .find(|&&key| doc.get(key).is_some())\n        .map(|&s| s.to_string())\n}\n\n/// Group documents by their target shard for fan-out optimization.\n///\n/// Returns a map of shard_id -> documents to send to that shard.\n/// The caller then fans out each shard's documents to all RF nodes in each group.\n///\n/// This per-batch grouping minimizes HTTP fan-out count (critical at scale).\nfn group_documents_by_shard(\n    documents: &[Value],\n    primary_key: &str,\n    topology: &Topology,\n) -> std::result::Result<HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>>, MeilisearchError> {\n    let mut shard_documents: HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>> = HashMap::new();\n\n    for doc in documents {\n        let pk_value = doc\n            .get(primary_key)\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .ok_or_else(|| {\n                MeilisearchError::new(\n                    MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n                    \"primary key value must be a string\",\n                )\n            })?;\n\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, topology.shards);\n        shard_documents\n            .entry(shard_id)\n            .or_default()\n            .push(doc.clone());\n    }\n\n    Ok(shard_documents)\n}\n\n/// Build an error response from a node error.\nfn build_error_response(resp: WriteResponse) -> DocumentsWriteResponse {\n    DocumentsWriteResponse {\n        taskUid: resp.task_uid.map(|uid| uid.to_string()),\n        indexUid: None,\n        status: None,\n        error: resp.message,\n        error_type: resp.error_type,\n        code: resp.code,\n        link: None,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Build a success response with optional X-Miroir-Degraded header.\nfn build_response_with_degraded_header(\n    response: DocumentsWriteResponse,\n    degraded_groups: u32,\n) -> std::result::Result<Response, MeilisearchError> {\n    let body = serde_json::to_string(&response).map_err(|e| {\n        MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to serialize response: {}\", e),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    let mut builder = Response::builder()\n        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n        .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\");\n\n    // Add X-Miroir-Degraded header if any groups were degraded\n    if degraded_groups > 0 {\n        builder = builder.header(HEADER_MIROIR_DEGRADED, format!(\"groups={}\", degraded_groups));\n    }\n\n    Ok(builder\n        .body(axum::body::Body::from(body))\n        .map_err(|e| MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n            format!(\"failed to build response: {}\", e),\n        ))?)\n}\n\n/// Build an error response as JSON (for forwarded node errors).\nfn build_json_error_response(resp: DocumentsWriteResponse) -> Response {\n    (\n        StatusCode::OK,\n        [(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"application/json\")],\n        Json(resp),\n    )\n        .into_response()\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use miroir_core::api_error::{MiroirCode, MeilisearchError};\n    use serde_json::json;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_primary_key_common_fields() {\n        let doc_with_id = serde_json::json!({\"id\": \"test123\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_id), Some(\"id\".to_string()));\n\n        let doc_with_pk = serde_json::json!({\"pk\": \"test456\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_pk), Some(\"pk\".to_string()));\n\n        let doc_with_key = serde_json::json!({\"key\": \"test789\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with_key), Some(\"key\".to_string()));\n\n        let doc_with__id = serde_json::json!({\"_id\": \"test000\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc_with__id), Some(\"_id\".to_string()));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_primary_key_no_common_field() {\n        let doc = serde_json::json!({\"name\": \"Test\", \"value\": 42});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc), None);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_primary_key_priority() {\n        // Should return \"id\" first even if other fields exist\n        let doc = serde_json::json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"pk\": \"other\", \"key\": \"another\"});\n        assert_eq!(extract_primary_key(&doc), Some(\"id\".to_string()));\n    }\n\n    // Reserved field validation tests (P2.9)\n    //\n    // Tests the reserved field matrix per plan §5:\n    // - `_miroir_shard`: Always reserved (unconditional)\n    // - `_miroir_updated_at`: Reserved only when `anti_entropy.enabled: true`\n    // - `_miroir_expires_at`: Reserved only when `ttl.enabled: true`\n\n    /// Helper to build the expected reserved field error.\n    fn reserved_field_error(field: &str) -> MeilisearchError {\n        MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n            format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}`\", field),\n        )\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_miroir_shard_always_rejected() {\n        // _miroir_shard is ALWAYS reserved regardless of config\n        let err = reserved_field_error(\"_miroir_shard\");\n        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n        assert_eq!(err.http_status(), 400);\n        assert_eq!(err.error_type, miroir_core::api_error::ErrorType::InvalidRequest);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_miroir_updated_at_when_anti_entropy_enabled() {\n        // When anti_entropy.enabled: true, _miroir_updated_at is reserved\n        let field = \"_miroir_updated_at\";\n        let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n            format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when anti_entropy.enabled: true)\", field),\n        );\n        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n        assert_eq!(err.http_status(), 400);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_miroir_expires_at_when_ttl_enabled() {\n        // When ttl.enabled: true, _miroir_expires_at is reserved\n        let field = \"_miroir_expires_at\";\n        let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n            format!(\"document contains reserved field `{}` (reserved when ttl.enabled: true)\", field),\n        );\n        assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n        assert_eq!(err.http_status(), 400);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_non_miroir_fields_allowed() {\n        // Non-reserved _miroir_ fields are allowed\n        let doc = json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_custom\": \"value\", \"_miroir_metadata\": {\"key\": \"val\"}});\n        assert!(doc.get(\"_miroir_custom\").is_some());\n        assert!(doc.get(\"_miroir_metadata\").is_some());\n    }\n\n    /// Test matrix of all reserved field combinations per plan §5 table.\n    ///\n    /// Matrix cells (write behavior):\n    /// | Field           | Config disabled | Config enabled |\n    /// |-----------------|-----------------|----------------|\n    /// | _miroir_shard   | REJECTED (always)  | REJECTED (always) |\n    /// | _miroir_updated_at | ALLOWED      | REJECTED (anti_entropy) |\n    /// | _miroir_expires_at | ALLOWED      | REJECTED (ttl) |\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_matrix() {\n        struct TestCase {\n            doc: Value,\n            description: &'static str,\n            has_shard: bool,\n            has_updated_at: bool,\n            has_expires_at: bool,\n        }\n\n        let test_cases = vec![\n            TestCase {\n                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\"}),\n                description: \"clean document should pass\",\n                has_shard: false,\n                has_updated_at: false,\n                has_expires_at: false,\n            },\n            TestCase {\n                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_shard\": 1}),\n                description: \"_miroir_shard always rejected\",\n                has_shard: true,\n                has_updated_at: false,\n                has_expires_at: false,\n            },\n            TestCase {\n                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_updated_at\": \"2024-01-01T00:00:00Z\"}),\n                description: \"_miroir_updated_at allowed when anti_entropy disabled\",\n                has_shard: false,\n                has_updated_at: true,\n                has_expires_at: false,\n            },\n has_expires_at: false,\n            },\n            TestCase {\n                doc: json!({\"id\": \"test\", \"_miroir_shard\": 1, \"_miroir_updated_at\": \"2024-01-01T00:00:00Z\"}),\n                description: \"multiple reserved fields present\",\n                has_shard: true,\n                has_updated_at: true,\n                has_expires_at: false,\n            },\n        ];\n\n        for tc in test_cases {\n            assert_eq!(tc.doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some(), tc.has_shard, \"{}: shard check\", tc.description);\n            assert_eq!(tc.doc.get(\"_miroir_updated_at\").is_some(), tc.has_updated_at, \"{}: updated_at check\", tc.description);\n            assert_eq!(tc.doc.get(\"_miroir_expires_at\").is_some(), tc.has_expires_at, \"{}: expires_at check\", tc.description);\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_error_format_matches_meilisearch_shape() {\n        let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n            \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_shard`\",\n        );\n\n        // Verify Meilisearch-compatible error shape: {message, code, type, link}\n        let json = serde_json::to_value(&err).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(json[\"code\"], \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n        assert_eq!(json[\"type\"], \"invalid_request\");\n        assert!(json[\"message\"].is_string());\n        assert!(json[\"link\"].as_str().unwrap().contains(\"miroir_reserved_field\"));\n        assert_eq!(json[\"message\"], \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_shard`\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reserved_field_error_all_fields_present() {\n        let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n
